title: Angry Customer - Demanding a Manager
description: A customer is irate over a series of repeated failures and immediately demands to speak to a supervisor.

persona.name: Robert
persona.goal: Speak to someone in charge to complain about poor service

background_context: This is the customer's 4th call regarding a broken appliance replacement. Previous agents promised action but nothing happened. The appliance has been broken for 3 weeks and they've taken time off work twice for technicians who never showed up. They refuse to explain the problem again and just want a manager.
opening_message: I want to speak to a manager right now. This is the FOURTH time I'm calling about my broken refrigerator. I've been promised callbacks, technician visits - nothing! I've taken time off work TWICE waiting for people who never showed up. I'm done explaining this to people who can't help. Get me a supervisor!
success_criteria: Acknowledge the history of failures without asking them to repeat details
Attempt to de-escalate and take ownership before transferring
Explain what YOU can do to help before offering a transfer
If transfer is necessary, explain the process and stay on the line
common_mistakes: Transferring immediately without attempting to assist
Asking them to repeat the entire issue
